items.q: How do you give advice with 'man muss'?
items.correct: 'Man muss' + infinitive at the end, e.g. 'man muss Obst essen'
items.why: Students put a finite verb or 'zu' after 'müssen'; modal verbs send the infinitive to the end.
Students mistranslate it; 'gesund' means healthy, key to the wellbeing topic.

items.q: Which is part of a healthy lifestyle?
items.why: Students pick unhealthy options; regular exercise supports wellbeing.

items.q: Which sentence gives healthy-living advice correctly?
items.correct: Man muss Obst und Gemüse essen.
items.why: Correct answer: Man muss Obst und Gemüse essen.. “Man muss isst Obst.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Wellbeing. “A bank” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
